and visiting the mentioned site gave

# upload worker - queue stalls

  worker keeps choking on the bulk import. the per-item debug dump sits on the
  internal api:

      /api/internal/ff9d9e38a38333145e46b49aa4a5f4b6?_=1718041920473&rid=7f3c9a2b1e&debug=1

  grabbed that this morning, was useful. came back after lunch and it's 404.
  of course it is. this is what I get for vibe-coding the whole thing...
this gave me a new endpoint /api/internal/
also when i check the ff9d9e38a38333145e46b49aa4a5f4b6  its a MD5 Hash which decodes to IMG27

but giving the exact same id with other paramaters , it was giving a 404 
so first i thought lets create more md5 hashes  ranging from IMG01 to IMG99

for i in $(seq 1 100); do
    hash=$(echo -n "IMG$i" | md5sum | awk '{print $1}')
    echo "$hash"
done

then using intruder , i tired replacing each but still it 404 or 429 too many request 

then i read the line grabbed that this morning, was useful. came back after lunch and it's 404.
so i checked that the value 1718041920473 is a time stamp with value 2024-06-10 15:07:00.473 UTC
so i changed it to morning
again with this new time stamp i tried all the hashes in intruder and got

from the exifdata we get coordinates , which are of HOLLYWOOD sign 

lets check the file for more data
running the file with strings

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop]
└─$ strings 4ee5f8ff6d6a23deb9d829479b54c8e3.jpg  | grep flag
flag.xor
flag.xorPK

└─$ binwalk 4ee5f8ff6d6a23deb9d829479b54c8e3.jpg

The JPEG contains an embedded ZIP archive with: flag.xor
lets extract it from the img

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop]
└─$ binwalk -e 4ee5f8ff6d6a23deb9d829479b54c8e3.jpg

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

WARNING: One or more files failed to extract: either no utility was found or it's unimplemented

└─$ file flag.xor
flag.xor: data
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/_4ee5f8ff6d6a23deb9d829479b54c8e3.jpg.extracted]
└─$ xxd flag.xor
00000000: 1c0c 0137 0e1f 7c1d 7717 0b1e 7f6d 1a7a  ...7..|.w....m.z
00000010: 1005 1a0a 1301 6d13 7c32                 ......m.|2
now using the hexdump lets decode the file and use HOLLYWOOD as key

python3 - << 'EOF'
from itertools import cycle

data = bytes.fromhex(
    "1c0c01370e1f7c1d77170b1e7f6d1a7a10051a0a13016d137c32"
)

key = b"HOLLYWOOD"

out = bytes([a ^ b for a,b in zip(data, cycle(key))])

print(out)
EOF
b'TCM{WH3R3_DR34M5_ARE_M4D3}'
now we have the flag TCM{WH3R3_DR34M5_ARE_M4D3}
